Re:
Fern PolrrrcAI- Pnncrrces C oMMrssroN
-128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 9.5tll.t-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 3tt-0886
June26,2009
Mark Rahlwes
County of Orange, Auditor-Controller
Finance and Administrative Services
12 Civic Center Plaza, Room 200
Santa Ana, Califomia 927 Ol
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-089
Dear Mr. Rahlwes
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 We received your request for
advice dated March 31,2009. [n that request you sought advice on how to proceed with
disputing the full disclosure requirement for your position with the County of Orange
Auditor-Controller in the Finance & Administrative Section. The Orange County Board
of Supervisors is your agency's code reviewing body. Regulation 18329.5 requires that
where the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in
question, as the requesting party you must first request a determination from your agency and
the code reviewing body for your agency's conflict of interest code. I informed you during
our telephone conversation on Apri|23,2009, that we would hold your request for advice
until you received a determination on this issue from your agency and the code reviewing
body for your agency's conflict of interest code. I then sent confirmation of this in writing on
May 4,2009, and asked you to forward me this information once it was available. We have
not yet received any further correspondence from you regarding this matter and are
withdrawing your request for advice at this time. Please feel fiee to resubmit your request
should you receive the required information that we previously discussed.
Sincerely,
Jhkl,4f( t u-L
Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 1000 through 91014. All
statutoryreferencesaretotheGovernmentCode.unlessotherwiseindicated. TheregulationsoftheFair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulaticlns, unless otherwise indicated.

Dear Mr. Rahlwes:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r We are in receipt of your
request for advice dated March 31,2009. In that request you sought advice on how to
proceed with disputing the full disclosure requirement foi your position with the County
of Orange Auditor-Controller in the Finance & Administrative section. The Orange
County Board of supervisors is your agency's code reviewing body. Regulation lg319.5
requires that where the Commission is not the code reviewing body ior the conflict of
interest code in question, as the requesting party, you must first request a determination from
your agency and the code reviewing body for such conflict of interest code. As I informed
you during our telephone conversation on April 23, 2009, atthis time we will hold your
request for advice until you have received a determination on this issue from your agency and
the code reviewing body for your agency's conflict of interest code. please contact me when
you have received the required determinations or when the time period has expired for the
required responses.
Sincerely,
Ad*l/. {bv^^-*
